source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

event.top_event: Worker struck by make-up tongs during tripping operations
event.summary: On May 10, 2015, during back-reaming while tripping out of the hole operations on the Hercules 300 jackup rig, a Floorhand was struck on his right arm by a set of make-up tongs which failed to remain secured to the drill pipe. The Floorhand was medically evacuated and later determined to have a radial fracture on his right arm. Root causes included inadequate Job Safety Analysis, lack of established safe standing zones, inefficient crew communication, absence of required supervision, and equipment failure of the Top Drive grabber dies.
recommendations: All JSA's need to be reviewed and verified to insure all hazards associated with job tasks have been identified
While utilizing tongs, an area on the rig floor needs to be designated for Floorhands to safely stand after the connection has been made for all operations utilizing tongs
Discussions during safety meetings should emphasize the importance of good working communications between crew members during operations
Stop Work Authority should have been utilized to assure the injured Floorhand was clear of the tongs prior to breaking and spinning out of a connection
Supervision from experienced personnel who are not responsible for a specific task during back-reaming operations can help prevent undesired events with potential of injury incidents

hazards.name: Rotating and moving drill pipe and tongs during tripping operations
Equipment failure of Top Drive grabber dies

threats.name: Make-up tongs fail to remain secured to drill pipe and swing unimpeded
Personnel positioned in hazardous area during tong operations
Top Drive grabber dies fail to provide necessary grip

consequences.name: Radial fracture to worker's right arm

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for back-reaming operations
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and establish safe work procedures

supporting_text: The initial Job Safety Analysis (JSA) conducted, May 10, 2015, did not include a proper analysis of the hazards associated with back-reaming out of the hole operations
Procedures for back-reaming out of the hole operations were not adequate and failed to incorporate safety measures to prevent this type of injury occurrence

controls.name: Establishment of safe standing zone for personnel during tong operations
controls.barrier_role: Keep personnel out of hazardous area where tongs can break free

supporting_text: A safe standing zone after the make-up tongs were connected was not established for the Floorhand who was injured
All rig floor crew members participating in the back-reaming operation failed to insure all personnel were in a safe location after securing make-up tongs to the drill pipe

controls.name: Crew communication during tripping operations
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all crew members are aware of hazards and safe positions

supporting_text: There was inefficient communication between crew members assigned to the task

controls.name: Supervision by Toolpusher during first ten stands of tripping
controls.barrier_role: Ensure compliance with procedures and safe positioning of personnel

supporting_text: As stated in the Tripping Practices (7.1.3), the Toolpusher should be present on the rig floor when pulling the first ten stands at a minimum. The Driller did not wait for the Toolpusher to arrive on the rig floor prior to start of operations
Due to the lack of onsite supervision, the Toolpusher was unable to completely insure all rig floor personnel were in their proper locations for safe operations

controls.name: Driller verification of personnel positioning before breaking connection
controls.barrier_role: Ensure Floorhand is in proper position prior to backing out connection

supporting_text: The Driller in particular failed to ensure the Floorhand was in the proper position prior to backing out of the connection while the tongs were being utilized on the rig floor

controls.name: Stop Work Authority utilization
controls.barrier_role: Halt operations when safety concerns identified or personnel not in safe position

supporting_text: Stop Work Authority should have been utilized to insure the Floorhand was clear of the tongs prior to breaking and spinning out of the connection

controls.name: Equipment inspection of Top Drive grabber dies
controls.barrier_role: Ensure equipment is functioning properly per manufacturer specifications

supporting_text: Equipment associated with any job task should be inspected as per the procedures, policies in place, and manufacturer specifications. The Top Drive grabber dies failed to provide the necessary grip to back out the Top Drive from the first stand of drill pipe pulled out of the hole

controls.name: Crew awareness and observation of safety issues
controls.barrier_role: Identify and report unsafe conditions and complacency

supporting_text: Experienced crew members were not taking the necessary time to review procedures, JSA's, and company policies thoroughly for routine operations. Experienced crew members were not observing others for safety issues due to complacency with routine operations

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
